2603.18482.title: The Truncation Blind Spot: How Decoding Strategies Systematically Exclude Human-Like Token Choices
2603.18482.paper_summary: Standard decoding strategies for text generation, including top-k, nucleus sampling, and contrastive search, select tokens based on likelihood, restricting selection to high-probability regions. Human language production operates differently: tokens are chosen for communicative appropriateness rather than statistical frequency. This mismatch creates a truncation blind spot: contextually appropriate but statistically rare tokens remain accessible to humans yet unreachable by likelihood-based decoding. We hypothesize this contributes to the detectability of machine-generated text. Analyzing over 1.8 million texts across eight language models, five decoding strategies, and 53 hyperparameter configurations, we find that 8-18% of human-selected tokens fall outside typical truncation boundaries. Simple classifiers trained on predictability and lexical diversity achieve remarkable detection rates. Crucially, neither model scale nor architecture correlates strongly with detectability; truncation parameters account for most variance. Configurations achieving low detectability often produce incoherent text, indicating that evading detection and producing natural text are distinct objectives. These findings suggest detectability is enhanced by likelihood-based token selection, not merely a matter of model capability.
2603.18482.paper_summary_zh: 標準的文本生成解碼策略，包括 top-k、nucleus 取樣和對比搜索，根據可能性選擇標記，將選擇限制在高概率區域。人類語言產出運作方式不同：標記是根據交際適當性而非統計頻率來選擇的。這種不匹配產生了一個截斷盲點：在上下文上適當但在統計上稀有的標記對人類仍然可訪問，但對基於可能性的解碼則無法觸及。我們假設這有助於機器生成文本的可檢測性。在分析了超過 180 萬篇文本、八種語言模型、五種解碼策略和 53 種超參數配置後，我們發現 8-18% 的人類選擇標記超出了典型的截斷邊界。基於可預測性和詞彙多樣性訓練的簡單分類器達到了顯著的檢測率。關鍵是，模型的規模和架構與可檢測性之間並沒有強烈的相關性；截斷參數佔據了大多數變異。實現低可檢測性的配置通常會產生不連貫的文本，這表明逃避檢測和生成自然文本是不同的目標。這些發現表明，可檢測性是通過基於可能性的標記選擇來增強的，而不僅僅是模型能力的問題。
2603.18482.author: Esteban Garces Arias et.al.
2603.18482.authors: Esteban Garces Arias, Nurzhan Sapargali, Christian Heumann, Matthias Aßenmacher
